title: The structure of gauge invariant Gaussian quantum operations on finite Fermion systems
abstract: Let ${\mathcal H}_1$ be a finite dimensional complex Hilbert space. Let $ψ\mapsto Z(ψ)$ be a canonical anti-commutation relations (CAR) field over ${\mathcal H}_1$ acting irreducibly on a Hilbert space ${\mathord{\mathscr K}}$. The $*$-algebra ${\mathscr A}_{{\mathcal H}_1}$ generated by the $Z(ψ)$, $ψ\in {\mathcal H}_1$, is simply all operators on ${\mathscr K}$.   However, the CAR field endows ${\mathscr A}_{{\mathcal H}_1}$ with additional structure, and we are concerned with quantum operations whose acting in harmony with this structure. In particular, there is a gauge automorphism group   generated by ``second quantizing'' $ψ\mapsto e^{it}ψ$. The fixed point algebra of the gauge group, ${\mathscr G}_{{\mathcal H}_1}$, is a sub-algebra of ${\mathscr A}_{{\mathcal H}_1}$ studied by Araki and Wyss. It contains the density matrices of an important class of states, the gauge invariant Gaussian states, ${\mathfrak S}_{GIG}$.   Our focus is on semigroups $\{e^{t{\mathscr L}}\}_{t\geq 0}$ of quantum operations on ${\mathscr A}_{{\mathcal H}_1}$ that map ${\mathfrak S}_{GIG}$ into itself. Each $e^{t{\mathscr L}}$ is one-to-one, and our first main result is a structure theorem forsuch quantum operations on ${\mathscr G}_{{\mathcal H}_1}$ that map ${\mathfrak S}_{GIG}$ into itself.   We apply this to study semigroups of quantum operations on ${\mathscr G}_{{\mathcal H}_1}$ that map ${\mathfrak S}_{GIG}$ into itself. Our second main result is a structure theorem showing that they are parameterized by pairs $(G,A)$ where $G$ is a contraction semigroup generator on ${\mathcal H}_1$, and $0 \leq A \leq -G -G^*$. We then show that each of these semigroups has a natural extension to the full CAR algebra ${\mathscr A}_{{\mathcal H}_1}$. Further results are obtained under further assumptions on the pair $(G,A)$.
summary: This paper provides a complete structural characterization of quantum operations and semigroups that preserve gauge-invariant Gaussian states in finite Fermion systems. It proves that one-to-one operations are determined by specific symbol maps and shows that such operations can be naturally extended to the full CAR algebra. The results establish a precise correspondence between GIG semigroups and pairs of operators acting on the single-particle space.
main_problem: The paper aims to characterize the structure of quantum operations (CPTP maps) and semigroups on finite-dimensional Fermion systems that preserve the set of gauge-invariant Gaussian (GIG) states.
main_result: The author provides a structure theorem for one-to-one quantum operations and semigroups, showing they are parameterized by pairs (G, A) where G is a contraction semigroup generator and 0 <= A <= -G - G*, and demonstrates how these operations extend to the full CAR algebra.
method: The study utilizes the algebraic framework of the Canonical Anti-commutation Relations (CAR), second quantization, symbol maps for Gaussian states, and tools from operator theory such as the Evans-Hugenholtz-Kadison construction and modular theory.
model_or_system: The system consists of finite-dimensional Fermion systems defined by a single-particle Hilbert space H1 and a corresponding Fock space K, governed by the CAR algebra and a gauge automorphism group.
key_observables: The number operator (N), the symbol (Q) representing the covariance/two-point function, and the particle-hole automorphism.
important_parameters: The dimension of the single-particle space, the contraction semigroup generator G, and the operator A satisfying the stability condition.
main_assumptions: The primary structure theorem assumes the quantum operations are one-to-one; the study is restricted to finite-dimensional Fermion systems.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the algebraic framework of the CAR field and gauge structure, proceeds to classify GIG states via symbols, establishes structure theorems for one-to-one quantum operations and semigroups, and concludes with results on the extension of these maps to the full CAR algebra and the properties of conditional expectations.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant to open quantum systems and many-body dynamics as it provides a rigorous classification of noise processes (quantum operations) that preserve the Gaussian structure of Fermionic systems, which is a cornerstone of many-body physics and quantum information processing.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
